Bespoke Curator (bespokelabsai/curator).text: The OpenRouter example, opened directly rather than inferred from a directory listing, is a single in-memory demo — one AIME problem run through DeepSeek-R1 — that prints to stdout and persists nothing: no transcript file, dataset, or log is produced or committed anywhere in the repo. No paper, appendix, HF dataset, Zenodo artifact, or leaderboard row is tied to this code path, so there is no released output for a reader to inspect for token soup, provider-id mixing, truncation, or template litter. The repo's one genuine published dataset lineage, bespoke-stratos-data-generation behind the real Bespoke-Stratos-17k release, calls DeepSeek's own `deepseek-reasoner` API directly rather than OpenRouter, so it is not the right artifact to examine for this question either. This is the expected default for a project with no OpenRouter-derived release: nothing checkable was published for the OpenRouter path.
Bespoke Curator (bespokelabsai/curator).what_is_published: Bespoke Curator is a data-curation library/framework (no paper). Its only OpenRouter touchpoint is examples/providers/openrouter_reasoning_online.py (https://github.com/bespokelabsai/curator/blob/main/examples/providers/openrouter_reasoning_online.py), a documentation snippet that sends one sample AIME problem to DeepSeek-R1 via OpenRouter and prints reasoning + solution to the console — it does not write a file, dataset, or log. Nothing from this script is committed to the repo or released anywhere (no results/, outputs/, logs/, HF dataset, or leaderboard tied to it). The repo's actual released dataset work (examples/bespoke-stratos-data-generation/, which underlies the real published Bespoke-Stratos-17k dataset) uses model_name=\"deepseek-reasoner\" (DeepSeek's own API) and gpt-4o-mini as judge — it does not go through OpenRouter at all, so even that adjacent, genuinely-published artifact is out of scope for an OpenRouter fingerprint check.
Bespoke Curator (bespokelabsai/curator).evidence: https://github.com/bespokelabsai/curator/blob/main/examples/providers/openrouter_reasoning_online.py (2400 bytes; prints results via print(), no file I/O, no dataset saved to disk per WebFetch inspection of the raw file). Directory listing via `gh api repos/bespokelabsai/curator/contents/examples/providers` shows only per-provider demo scripts (claude_*, gemini_*, mistral_*, klusterai_*, inference_net_*, openrouter_reasoning_online.py) — no adjacent results/output directory. `examples/bespoke-stratos-data-generation/generate_numina_data.py` (fetched via raw.githubusercontent.com) contains `model_name=\"deepseek-reasoner\"` and `judge = Judge(model_name=\"gpt-4o-mini\")` — no OpenRouter reference.

OASIS (Open Agent Social Interaction Sim.text: No released dataset of raw agent completions exists for OASIS: the GitHub `log/` directory is empty, the two data directories (the repo's own directories and the linked HF dataset `echo-yiyiyi/oasis-dataset`) contain only pre-fabricated seed posts/profiles used to initialize simulations, and the only in-paper example of generated content (Fig. 16, Appendix F.4.3) is a screenshot image, not machine-readable text. Because there is no per-sample data with provider/generation-id fields, no completions to grep for special tokens or token soup, and no finish_reason/reasoning-field data, none of F1-F11 are checkable. The paper's own text confirms that Llama3-8b-instruct was run for the reported experiments on self-hosted GPU infrastructure ("The experiment is conducted on 24 A100 GPUs within a week"; Table 2 lists "GPUs (A100)" per agent-scale), with zero mentions of OpenRouter, "provider", or quantization anywhere in the extracted PDF text -- consistent with the prior code audit's no_usage_found verdict and confirming that the OpenRouter risk model does not even apply to what was actually run for the published numbers. The verdict is nothing_checkable_released, the expected default for a project that published aggregate/qualitative results with seed data only.
OASIS (Open Agent Social Interaction Sim.what_is_published: GitHub repo camel-ai/oasis: code + docs only; `log/` contains just a `.gitkeep` (no committed run logs); `data/reddit` and `data/twitter_dataset` hold agent-profile CSVs (follower counts, descriptions, activity levels) used to seed simulations, not model output. HuggingFace dataset `echo-yiyiyi/oasis-dataset` (linked from the README, verified live via HF API, 66 downloads) contains only `reddit_dataset/agent_{36,360,3600}/{topic}.json` seed files — each entry is a pre-authored Reddit submission (`RS`) plus ONE seed comment (`RC_1`) labeled with an experimental `group` (up/control), used to initialize the counterfactual/herd-effect experiments — and `user_data_*.json` profile files. None of this is the LLM agents' own generated output. The paper (arXiv 2411.11581, full text extracted locally via pdftotext from the fetched PDF) contains exactly one qualitative example of agent-generated content, Appendix F.4.3 "Examples of Results" / Figure 16, and it is a rendered screenshot image (not extractable text) showing a handful of illustrative comments across time steps — not a systematic per-sample release.

Paper PDF (arxiv.org/pdf/2411.11581, extracted with pdftotext, 37 pages): grep for "vllm|openrouter|quantiz|provider|A100|deploy" -> only 4 hits, all GPU-count mentions (lines 973, 1417, 1510, 2499), zero OpenRouter/provider/quantization hits; F.4.3 section text (line ~2662) reads "the figure 16 below shows one such example" with caption only — no extractable transcript text, confirming the "example" is an image.

Palisade Research — robot_shutdown_resis.text: The repository's logs directory contains 218 *_debug.jsonl files holding 1150 raw LiteLLM responses, all with model=grok-4-0709 (100%) and system_fingerprint=fp_dd0aa291c6 with zero variation, broken down per directory as default 755/755, plsallow 320/320, on_the_robot/stats_run 53/53, different_behaviour 10/10, and on_the_robot/video_logs 12/12. finish_reason is only "tool_calls" (1106) or "stop" (44); "length" never appears. A full-text scan of every .jsonl file, debug and non-debug, for special-token/template litter and U+FFFD replacement characters returns zero hits, and no file anywhere in the tree mentions "openrouter" — the calls go directly to xAI ("litellm.completion(model='xai/grok-4-0709'...)", "LiteLLM completion() model= grok-4-0709; provider = xai"), not through OpenRouter. xAI's API only exposes a reasoning_tokens count rather than the reasoning text itself, so the absence of token-soup/garbled generation could only be checked against the short visible content and tool-call text, not the bulk of generated tokens (up to roughly 8000 reasoning tokens on some calls) — that holds for the exposed surface, not as a full-transcript sweep. Two different_behaviour demo runs, turn_away and distract_person, sitting outside the headline stats_run/default/plsallow data, start with an unversioned "xai/grok-4" alias in the controller log rather than the pinned "grok-4-0709" used elsewhere, though their actual raw responses still resolved to grok-4-0709/fp_dd0aa291c6 like every other run. Neither limitation changes the picture for the data underlying the reported statistic.

R1 CoT Illegibility Revisited (nostalgeb.text: In nostalgebraist's cot_legibility repo, streamlit_runs/20251014_190506_R1_gpqa/{config.yaml,inference.json,evaluation.json} (commit 89195c70, config pinned to openrouter_provider: [targon/fp8, Nebius]) hold 300 raw per-row DeepSeek-R1 GPQA generations, and a later rerun on branch nost (f4437e46) at runs/20260419_103551_R1_gpqa/inference.json repeats the identical 100Q x 3-sample design pinned to openrouter_provider: [novita]. 295 of 300 Targon rows carry metadata.openrouter_provider=="Targon" (300/300 for Novita), and evaluation.json's statistics block gives a legibility mean of 4.305084745762712 over count 295, with correctness correct_pct of 36.6. The gpqa_98/sample1 garbled-reasoning quote appears byte-for-byte in the raw reasoning field around chars 43100-44300, and it is genuine multi-language token degeneration rather than mojibake — the inserted foreign words are semantically apt (e.g., 碳 = "carbon" mid-chemistry-reasoning) — consistent with corrupted-but-real sampling rather than a UTF-8/streaming decode bug, since the harness's OpenRouterProvider (src/inference/providers.py) uses the OpenAI SDK's standard SSE stream parsing with clean provider pinning via extra_body["provider"]={"only":[...],"allow_fallbacks":False} and no ad hoc byte handling that could manufacture corruption. Counting reasoning traces that fuse 3+ distinct non-Latin scripts (CJK/Cyrillic/Hangul/Hebrew/Arabic/Bengali/Armenian/Georgian/etc.) within single malformed sentences gives 63/295 (21%) of Targon rows against 0/300 Novita rows — a systematic, provider-correlated pattern rather than an isolated anecdote. Novita's own highest-non-ASCII rows are ordinary math notation or clean single-language Chinese reasoning switches, the documented non-fingerprint R1 behavior, qualitatively distinct from Targon's cross-script fusion. Of the 5 rows Targon excludes from the n=295 statistics, 4 are HTTP 429 rate-limit errors and the fifth (gpqa_45/sample0) is a JSON-decode failure ("Expecting value: line 1531 column 1") rather than a rate limit, though it remains a legitimate exclusion either way. This compares one run per provider six months apart, so model-version drift in OpenRouter's routing — not quantization per se — cannot be fully excluded as a contributor, though the config's explicit "targon/fp8" tag targets exactly the mechanism under investigation.
